use std::fs::File;
use std::io::Write;

// GOOD: use transactions or cleanup on failure
fn write_and_backup(data: &[u8], path: &str, backup_path: &str) -> Result<(), Error> {
    // Write to temp file first, then rename
    let temp_path = format!("{}.tmp", path);
    let mut file = File::create(&temp_path)?;
    file.write_all(data)?;
    file.flush()?;
    drop(file);

    let mut backup = File::create(backup_path)?;
    backup.write_all(data)?;
    backup.flush()?;
    drop(backup);

    std::fs::rename(&temp_path, path)?;
    Ok(())
}

fn transfer_funds(from: &mut Account, to: &mut Account, amount: u64) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let original_from = from.balance;
    let original_to = to.balance;

    from.balance -= amount;
    if let Err(e) = from.save() {
        from.balance = original_from;
        return Err(e);
    }

    to.balance += amount;
    if let Err(e) = to.save() {
        from.balance = original_from;
        from.save()?;
        to.balance = original_to;
        return Err(e);
    }

    Ok(())
}
